prevalence.value: 10-20% in adults >60 years (higher in low-income settings)
prevalence.source: Geriatrics epidemiology syntheses (frailty) + Indian aging cohort context
prevalence.trend: increasing with rapid aging and multimorbidity

vulnerablePopulations: older adults with multiple chronic diseases
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Frailty phenotype or frailty index; screening tools used in some geriatric clinics

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: tens of millions of older adults
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Low to moderate; dependent on nutrition and exercise support availability

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Often recognized after functional decline; limited geriatric services
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Exercise, nutrition supplementation, deprescribing, fall prevention, and social support; integrate with NCD care
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited geriatric workforce and high malnutrition burden in some settings
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Family caregiving and community programs can support functional interventions where available
